citations.narrative:
The surfaces of the sheltered housing facilities are not being maintained impervious to moisture. There are four primary
enclosures currently housing 8 adult dogs that have vertical support poles on the northern end of the outdoor portion of
the enclosure that are made of wood. These poles have large areas of chipped or missing paint. The area with missing
paint is exposing the raw unsealed wood underneath that appears green in appearance due to moisture exposure.
Surfaces made of unsealed wood cannot be properly cleaned or sanitized when permeable to moisture. The licensee
must ensure that all walls, boxes, houses, dens, and other surfaces in contact with animals are impervious to moisture.
Correct by 18 May 2026

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The primary enclosures are not being sufficiently cleaned. A primary enclosure housing 2 adult dogs was observed with
approximately 20-30 piles of excreta throughout the outdoor portion of the enclosure. The excreta observed in the primary
enclosure varied in appearance from fresh with a moist appearance to dry with two of the piles appearing very dry and
chalky white in color indicating feces is not being removed daily as required.
Failure to remove excreta from primary enclosures daily can result in an accumulation of feces which can lead to
increased disease hazards, discomfort for the animals, increased odors, and/or attract unwanted pests.
Excreta and other waste must be removed from primary enclosures daily to prevent an excessive accumulation of feces,
to prevent soiling of the dogs contained in the primary enclosures, and to reduce disease hazards, insects, pests, and
odors.
Correct by 4 March 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.3(e)(1)(iii) 
   
Sheltered housing facilities. 
The surfaces of the sheltered housing facilities are not being maintained impervious to moisture. There are four primary 
enclosures currently housing 8 adult dogs that have vertical support poles on the northern end of the outdoor portion of 
the enclosure that are made of wood. These poles have large areas of chipped or missing paint. The area with missing 
paint is exposing the raw unsealed wood underneath that appears green in appearance due to moisture exposure.  
 
Surfaces made of unsealed wood cannot be properly cleaned or sanitized when permeable to moisture. The licensee 
must ensure that all walls, boxes, houses, dens, and other surfaces in contact with animals are impervious to moisture.  
 
Correct by 18 May 2026 
3.11(a) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The primary enclosures are not being sufficiently cleaned. A primary enclosure housing 2 adult dogs was observed with 
approximately 20-30 piles of excreta throughout the outdoor portion of the enclosure. The excreta observed in the primary 
enclosure varied in appearance from fresh with a moist appearance to dry with two of the piles appearing very dry and 
chalky white in color indicating feces is not being removed daily as required.  
 
Failure to remove excreta from primary enclosures daily can result in an accumulation of feces which can lead to 
increased disease hazards, discomfort for the animals, increased odors, and/or attract unwanted pests. 
 
Excreta and other waste must be removed from primary enclosures daily to prevent an excessive accumulation of feces, 
to prevent soiling of the dogs contained in the primary enclosures, and to reduce disease hazards, insects, pests, and 
odors.  
 
Correct by 4 March 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
